- Architecture centers on staged process lifecycle, selectable Qt wrapper, selectable web content backend, declarative/programmatic configuration, and command-driven UI.
- Top-level decisions: [[entry-lifecycle]], [[qt-binding-abstraction]], [[web-backend]], [[configuration-model]], [[command-ui-boundaries]].

items: - Launchers delegate to qutebrowser.qutebrowser.main().

- Windows are shown after tabs/start pages are opened.

items: - User actions are exposed as commands registered by decorators.
- Command metadata is inferred from Python function signatures, docstrings, annotations, and cmdutils.argument metadata.
- CommandRunner parses command text, replaces variables from the active tab/window, runs commands, and records repeat/macro state.
- MainWindow composes tabbed browser, downloads, status bar, completion, key hints, prompts, messages, mode manager, and per-window command runner.
- Command-facing objects are registered in scoped object registries rather than passed through global UI references.
- Browser commands are dispatched through a per-window CommandDispatcher tied to the tabbed browser.

items: - UI and command changes persist to generated autoconfig.yml.

- Generated config.py output can write current customizations or commented defaults.

items: Extension-visible symbols are re-exported through qutebrowser.api modules.
Command registration uses decorators that preserve the original handler function.
Command arguments derive from Python function signatures, annotations, defaults, and explicit @cmdutils.argument metadata.

Messages expose only info, warning, and error status-bar helpers.

Tab, web element, inspector, key mode, click target, JS world, and init context types are collected in apitypes.

items: Request interception is a global ordered list of interceptor callables.
Each interceptor receives a Request object with first-party URL, request URL, blocked flag, and optional resource type.
Blocking is performed by mutating Request.is_blocked through Request.block().
Redirection is a method on Request and can raise RedirectException.
Unsupported redirect methods can be ignored explicitly with ignore_unsupported=True.
Resource types mirror Qt WebEngine request resource types and include websocket, json, and unknown fallbacks.
The public qutebrowser.api.interceptor module re-exports Request, InterceptorType, and ResourceType while delegating registration to qutebrowser.extensions.interceptors.

items: Ad blocking is implemented as components behind the extension API rather than as direct browser core code.
Host-file blocking and Brave adblock-engine blocking are separate components sharing one user update command.
content.blocking.method selects host, Brave adblock, both, or automatic behavior.
The host blocker is used for auto mode when the adblock dependency is not usable.
The Brave blocker is optional and leaves its global blocker unset when the adblock dependency is missing or outdated.
Both blockers register request interceptors during init hooks.
The shared :adblock-update command updates both available blocker implementations.

items: Userscripts are external executables launched through :spawn --userscript or hint userscript actions.
Userscripts receive qutebrowser state through QUTE_* environment variables.
Userscripts send qutebrowser commands by writing lines to QUTE_FIFO.
POSIX command delivery uses a named pipe monitored with QSocketNotifier for immediate execution.
Windows command delivery uses a regular temporary file read after the userscript exits.
Userscript lookup searches data/userscripts, system data/userscripts, config/userscripts, or accepts absolute paths.
The page HTML and plaintext are dumped asynchronously before process start and exposed as temporary file paths.
Userscript output can be sent to process messages or process output pages through the same GUIProcess infrastructure as :spawn.
